2605.03512.title: Brainrot: Deskilling and Addiction are Overlooked AI Risks
2605.03512.paper_summary: The scope of AI safety and alignment work in generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) has so far mostly been limited to harms related to: (a) discrimination and hate speech, (b) harmful/inappropriate (violent, sexual, illegal) content, (c) information hazards, and (d) use cases related to malicious actors, such as cybersecurity, child abuse, and chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear threats. The public conversation around AI, on the other hand, has also been focusing on threats to our cognition, mental health, and welfare at large, related to over-relying on new technologies, most recently, those related to GenAI. Examples include deskilling associated with cognitive offloading and the atrophy of critical thinking as a result of over-reliance on GenAI systems, and addiction associated with attachment and dependence on GenAI systems. Such risks are rarely addressed, if at all, in the AI safety and alignment literature. In this paper, we highlight and quantify this discrepancy and discuss some initial thoughts on how safety and alignment work could address cognitive and mental health concerns. Finally, we discuss how information campaigns and regulation can be used to mitigate such prominent risks.
2605.03512.paper_summary_zh: AI安全與對齊工作的範疇在生成人工智慧（GenAI）方面至今主要限於與以下相關的危害：(a) 歧視和仇恨言論，(b) 有害/不當（暴力、性、非法）內容，(c) 資訊危害，以及 (d) 與惡意行為者相關的使用案例，例如網絡安全、兒童虐待，以及化學、生物、放射性和核威脅。另一方面，公眾對AI的討論也集中在對我們的認知、心理健康和整體福祉的威脅上，這與過度依賴新技術有關，最近尤其是與GenAI相關的技術。例子包括與認知卸載相關的技能喪失，以及因過度依賴GenAI系統而導致的批判性思維萎縮，還有與對GenAI系統的依賴和依附相關的成癮。這些風險在AI安全與對齊文獻中很少被提及，即使有也不多。在本文中，我們突顯並量化這一差異，並討論一些初步想法，探討安全與對齊工作如何應對認知和心理健康問題。最後，我們討論如何利用資訊宣傳和監管來減輕這些突出的風險。
2605.03512.author: Ilias Chalkidis et.al.
